subject: Gaming
title: The Atari Video Game Burial
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Atari Video Game Burial usable.

hook.text: Atari buried millions of unsold E.T. cartridges in a New Mexico desert. It became gaming's most famous failure.

buildup.text: E.T. for the Atari 2600 was rushed to market in just five weeks. It was widely considered the worst game ever made.

discovery.text: Atari produced millions of copies that didn't sell. In 1983, they dumped them in a landfill and buried them.

twist.text: In 2014, excavators dug up the burial site and confirmed the urban legend was real. Cartridges were found.

climax.text: E.T.'s failure contributed to the 1983 video game crash. One bad game helped destroy an entire industry.

punchline.text: Millions of cartridges buried in desert sand. A legend confirmed after 30 years.

quiz.question: What was confirmed about the Atari burial site in 2014?
options: Excavators found the buried E.T. cartridges, confirming the urban legend
It contained Atari consoles, not games
